title: batch_execute runs many typed tools in one round trip with a batch-level gate

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: Live Editor + bridge running on the demo project. The active scene should be one you are willing to mutate transiently (the scenario creates and then destroys GameObjects). The batch_execute tool ships in the `core` group — no manage_tools activation needed. This scenario is the runnable confidence pass for the M27 Plan 4 batch gate; the per-step dispatch + deny-list + fail_fast semantics are asserted by BatchExecuteToolTests.

steps.title: Run a 3-step batch (create 2 cubes + 1 material) in one round trip
Expected outcomes (happy path)
items: batch.results[] has three entries, each status:"success" and carrying the nested tool's output (instanceId / assetPath)
gate.validation.passed is true and gate.delta.newErrors is 0 (one gate cycle for the whole batch)
the Batch tab in the bridge window shows the run with three Done entries
a single editor_undo reverts all three creations (one undo group for the batch)

steps.title: Run a 3-step batch where step 1 fails (fail_fast:true default)
Expected outcomes (fail_fast abort)
items: batch.results[0].status is "success", batch.results[1].status is "failed" with error.code "missing_parameter"
batch.results[2].status is "skipped" — the third step was NOT executed (no M27_Batch_Skipped GameObject exists)
agentNextSteps explains the partial-failure semantics + single editor_undo recovery

steps.title: Attempt to nest a blocked power tool (execute_csharp)
Expected outcomes (deny-list)
items: error.code is "batch_tool_not_invokable" — the whole batch is rejected before any step runs (clean rejection, no partial state)
error.message names execute_csharp and explains it must be a single top-level call

steps.title: Clean up the batch-created GameObjects + material

reset.note: Recommended scenario. The batch gate + fail_fast + deny-list contract is asserted by BatchExecuteToolTests; this runnable pass validates the live end-to-end round trip + the Batch tab progress + the single-undo-group recovery on a real Editor.
